behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at a module handoff boundary: three collectors must be wired and merged before calling summary generation. A unit test of one function can miss wiring omissions. Real OS/network/LLM behavior is not required if sources are stubbed.
behaviors.test_description: Run the report pipeline with three in-process source adapters returning distinct sentinel values; assert the payload sent to summary generation contains all three sections/values.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module error propagation and fallback rendering (source adapter -> aggregation -> report formatting). Stubbed failure from one adapter is sufficient to reproduce the real failure boundary; no real network or model output quality is required.
behaviors.test_description: Configure one source adapter to raise an availability error while others return valid data; assert report generation completes and includes a `data unavailable` marker only for the failed section.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on model judgment/output quality (faithful narrative representation), which a deterministic stub would hide. Rejected Integration because behavior text is about summary accuracy, not just routing/retries around the model call.
behaviors.test_description: Use real summary generation model/API on representative fixtures with known key values; score factual consistency (key metrics/issues/deployments preserved) and fail on omissions or hallucinated values.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is end-to-end infrastructure: real API calls and real email transport/inbox delivery. In-process fakes can hide auth, transport, formatting, and delivery issues. Rejected Agentic because this behavior is delivery-to-inbox correctness, not evaluation of LLM decision quality.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the scheduled job in a system environment against real/staging data endpoints and a real mailbox; assert one complete report email arrives at configured recipients with expected sections and attachments/body format.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure is orchestration/state handoff across local modules: send failure handling, persistence of unsent report, and retry logic on next run. This can be caught with in-process wiring plus local filesystem and a failing email adapter. Rejected System because real external email/network is not required to verify fallback+retry behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run job once with email adapter forced to fail; assert report is persisted locally. Run job again with adapter restored; assert persisted report is retried/sent and local pending artifact is cleared or marked delivered.
